witnesses.description: In a -> b -> c, the path from a to c is active with no conditioning.
In a -> b <- c and b -> d, conditioning on d opens the collider at b.

checks.claim: a and c are d-connected in the chain a -> b -> c with no conditioning.
checks.notes: The validator checks the DAG, the path, and the non-collider rule for an empty conditioning set.

checks.claim: a and c are d-connected in the chain a -> b -> c after conditioning on b.

data.certificate: The Boolean resource regression emits a DRAT refutation, elaborates it to LRAT, and checks both proof objects independently.

checks.notes: The validator enumerates skeleton paths, checks the conditioned non-collider DIMACS artifact, and the Rust regression confirms emitted DRAT and LRAT proof objects independently check.

checks.claim: a and c are d-connected in the fork a <- b -> c after conditioning on b.
checks.notes: The validator confirms the fork path is blocked because b is a conditioned non-collider.

checks.claim: a and c are d-connected in the collider a -> b <- c with no conditioning.

data.certificate: The Boolean resource regression emits a DRAT refutation, elaborates it to LRAT, and checks both proof objects independently.

checks.notes: The validator confirms the only path is blocked because b is an unconditioned collider with no conditioned descendant, checks the fixed DIMACS artifact, and the Rust regression confirms emitted DRAT and LRAT proof objects independently check.

checks.claim: a and c are d-connected when conditioning on a descendant of the collider.
checks.notes: The validator checks that conditioning on d opens the collider at b because d is a descendant of b.
